controls.title: API1:2023 — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA/IDOR)
controls.text: APIs that accept object identifiers from clients must verify the requesting user is authorised to access the specific object. Lack of per-object authorisation is the most common and impactful API vulnerability.
controls.evidence_expectation: BOLA/IDOR test cases with cross-account object access proof; affected endpoints and object types
controls.assessment_procedure: Map all endpoints that accept object IDs; authenticate as User A and attempt to access User B's objects; test with manipulated IDs (sequential, UUID swap, encoded variants); verify authorisation enforced server-side

controls.title: API2:2023 — Broken Authentication
controls.text: API authentication mechanisms are misconfigured or missing: weak tokens, no rate limiting on auth endpoints, predictable credentials, lack of MFA, or insecure token storage.
controls.evidence_expectation: Authentication endpoint testing results; token strength analysis; brute-force test evidence; credential stuffing resilience results
controls.assessment_procedure: Test auth endpoints for rate limiting; analyse token randomness and expiry; attempt credential stuffing; verify MFA enforced on sensitive operations; check for token leakage in logs or URLs

controls.title: API3:2023 — Broken Object Property Level Authorization
controls.text: APIs fail to filter object properties based on the user's access level, exposing sensitive fields in responses or allowing mass assignment of protected properties.
controls.evidence_expectation: Response body analysis showing over-exposed fields; mass assignment test results; role-based property access comparison
controls.assessment_procedure: Compare API responses across user roles; check for sensitive fields returned to unauthorised users; test mass assignment by injecting non-allowed properties in requests; verify server-side allowlisting

controls.title: API4:2023 — Unrestricted Resource Consumption
controls.text: APIs lack rate limiting or resource consumption controls, enabling DoS attacks through mass requests, large payload abuse, or long-running queries that exhaust server resources.
controls.evidence_expectation: Rate limiting test results; large payload acceptance results; resource consumption measurement under load
controls.assessment_procedure: Test request rate limits on all API endpoints; send oversized payloads; test with deeply nested JSON; measure response degradation; verify limits enforced per user/IP/API key

controls.title: API5:2023 — Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA)
controls.text: Administrative or privileged API functions are accessible to regular or unauthenticated users due to missing server-side authorisation checks on function-level access.
controls.evidence_expectation: Admin function access test results; role-bypassed function calls; privilege escalation evidence
controls.assessment_procedure: Map all API endpoints including administrative paths; authenticate as low-privilege user; attempt access to admin endpoints; test HTTP method switching (GET→POST→DELETE); test parameter-based role bypass

controls.title: API6:2023 — Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
controls.text: APIs that accept URLs or remote resource references without validation enable SSRF attacks, allowing attackers to reach internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, or internal network resources.
controls.evidence_expectation: SSRF proof-of-concept showing internal resource access; cloud metadata endpoint access evidence; DNS callback confirmation
controls.assessment_procedure: Identify all URL-accepting parameters; test with internal IP ranges, cloud metadata URLs (169.254.169.254), localhost; use Burp Collaborator/interactsh to confirm out-of-band SSRF; test protocol smuggling

controls.title: API7:2023 — Security Misconfiguration
controls.text: APIs expose sensitive information through default configurations, unnecessary HTTP methods, verbose error messages, missing security headers, or insecure CORS policies.
controls.evidence_expectation: Security headers audit; CORS policy analysis; error message content review; HTTP method enumeration results
controls.assessment_procedure: Review security headers; test CORS with arbitrary origins; verify error messages do not expose stack traces; check allowed HTTP methods; test for exposed API documentation (Swagger/OpenAPI) in production

controls.title: API8:2023 — Security Misconfiguration (Injection)
controls.text: API inputs passed to interpreters (SQL, OS, LDAP, GraphQL) without proper validation or sanitisation enable injection attacks that can lead to data exfiltration, authentication bypass, or remote code execution.
controls.evidence_expectation: Injection test results; payload responses showing successful injection; affected endpoint and parameter list
controls.assessment_procedure: Test all API inputs with injection payloads (SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, GraphQL); use automated scanning; verify input sanitisation applied server-side; test for GraphQL introspection and injection

controls.title: API9:2023 — Improper Inventory Management
controls.text: Outdated, undocumented, or shadow API versions remain accessible, exposing users to older vulnerabilities or bypassing security controls present in newer versions.
controls.evidence_expectation: API version discovery results; old/shadow API endpoint inventory; version comparison security gap analysis
controls.assessment_procedure: Enumerate all API versions accessible (/v1, /v2, /api/legacy); test whether security controls are equivalent across versions; check for undocumented endpoints via path fuzzing; review API gateway configuration

controls.title: API10:2023 — Unsafe Consumption of APIs
controls.text: Applications that integrate third-party APIs without proper validation of responses are vulnerable to supply-chain attacks where a compromised upstream API delivers malicious payloads.
controls.evidence_expectation: Third-party API integration review; response validation analysis; supply chain dependency audit
controls.assessment_procedure: Map all third-party API integrations; review whether responses are validated before use; check for SSRF risks in integration paths; assess whether upstream API credentials are stored securely; review dependency versions for known vulnerabilities
